title: Mesembryanthemum lacunatum
taxonomy: Mesembryanthemum lacunatum is classified within the family Aizoaceae, which is a group of succulent plants commonly known as the ice plants. This plant is a part of the genus Mesembryanthemum, which is characterized by its fleshy leaves and stems. Mesembryanthemum lacunatum is native to the Cape Provinces of South Africa. It is often found growing in sandy or rocky areas, and is known for its ability to thrive in dry conditions. The plant's classification also places it within the order Caryophyllales, a group of flowering plants that are known for their five-petaled flowers.
